chart.title: Identifies clusters of failures that repeatedly occur within short time windows. High counts indicate recurring incidents or unstable deployments.

search.query: `svc_failure_detect($index$)` | timechart span=15m sum(is_failure) AS failures BY service

chart.title: Estimates incident duration based on consecutive failure periods. Longer durations indicate outages; short durations indicate flapping.

search.query: `svc_failure_detect($index$)` | sort service _time | streamstats current=f last(is_failure) AS prev_failure BY service | eval incident_start = if(is_failure=1 AND prev_failure=0, _time, null()) | eval incident_end = if(is_failure=0 AND prev_failure=1, _time, null()) | stats earliest(incident_start) AS start latest(incident_end) AS end BY service | eval duration_min = round((end - start) / 60, 2) | where duration_min > 0 | bucket duration_min span=5 | stats count BY duration_min

table.title: Number of detected recovery transitions per service. High values may indicate restarts, retries, or automated recovery loops.

search.query: `svc_recovery_patterns($index$)` | eval recovery_severity = case( recovery_events >= 20, "HIGH", recovery_events >= 5, "MEDIUM", true(), "LOW" ) | sort - recovery_events

viz.title: Heatmap of failure frequency by hour of day. Useful for identifying batch jobs, cron schedules, traffic spikes, or maintenance windows causing incidents.

search.query: `svc_failure_detect($index$)` | eval hour=strftime(_time, "%H") | stats sum(is_failure) AS failures BY service hour

div.p: This dashboard helps identify recurring incident behavior and failure windows using historical log data. It is intended for post-incident review and long-term reliability improvement.

ul.li: An incident window is a period of consecutive failure events.
Repeated short windows indicate flapping or unstable dependencies.
Consistent failures at the same hour often indicate scheduled jobs or traffic spikes.
Random patterns suggest code or dependency instability.
Correlate with deployment or change timelines.
Feed insights into runbooks and alert tuning.
